message:
fix(e2e): scale load generation based on initial replica count (#527)

* fix(e2e): scale load generation based on initial replica count

Scale the number of load generation workers proportionally to the
initial replica count to ensure consistent load pressure per replica.

The base load (10 workers) is tuned for 2 replicas. When starting with
fewer replicas:
- 1 replica: 5 workers (half the load)
- 2 replicas: 10 workers (full load)

This prevents excessive scaling on clusters with limited GPUs. Without
this fix, starting with 1 replica under full load causes massive queue
buildup (131s TTFT observed) and the system scales to 7+ replicas to
catch up.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: use explicit rounding for load worker scaling calculation

Address Copilot review: use math.Round with floating-point arithmetic
to avoid integer division truncation when scaling load workers
proportionally to initial replicas.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
